page.title: Five Male and Three Female Deities

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:アマテラスとスサノオの誓約]][[ko:아마테라스와 스사노오의 서약]]
'''The Covenant of Amaterasu and Susanoo''' (アマテラスとスサノヲの誓約, ''Amaterasu to Susanoo no Ukei'') refers to the {{ill|Divination|en|Divination|qid=Q1043197|lt=Divination|1=Divination}} (誓約, ''ukei'') performed by {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (Amaterasu-Ōmikami) and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo-no-Mikoto) as recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.
== Summary == === Kojiki=== When {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} commanded {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to rule the seas, Susanoo instead cried out that he wanted to visit {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} in the {{ill|Land of Yomi|ja|黄泉の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Land of Yomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄泉|1=Yomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1142558}} (Yomi). His wailing caused great destruction to the heavens and earth. Angered, Izanagi banished him from the lands.
Susanoo decided to meet his sister, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, before heading to Yomi. As Susanoo ascended to the heavens, the mountains and rivers trembled. Fearing he had come to seize her land, Amaterasu armed herself for battle. Susanoo declared his innocence and suggested performing a ''ukei'' to prove his intentions.
The two deities stood on opposite sides of the {{ill|Heavenly River|ja|天の安河|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and conducted the ''ukei''. Amaterasu took Susanoo’s {{ill|Ten-Hand Sword|ja|十拳剣|1=Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten-Hand Sword|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十束剣}} (''Totsuka-no-Tsurugi''), broke it into three pieces, and washed them in the {{ill|Heavenly Sacred Well|ja|天の真名井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Sacred Well|1=Amenomanai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442122}} before chewing and spitting them out. From the mist of her breath, the following three goddesses, known as the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, were born:
* {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takirihime-no-Mikoto), also called Okitsushima-hime. * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto), also called Sayorihime. * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto).
Next, Susanoo took Amaterasu’s {{ill|Jewel Strings of Yasakani|ja|八尺の勾玉の五百箇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jewel Strings of Yasakani|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and performed the same ritual. From his breath, the following five male deities were born:
* {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天之忍穂耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}} (Ameno-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto). * {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天之菩卑能命|1=Ame no Hohi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohiboko|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}} (Amenohiboko-no-Mikoto). * {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津日子根命|1=Amatsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto). * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|活津彦根命|1=Ikutsuhikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イクツヒコネ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11147583}} (Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto). * {{ill|Kumanokusubi|ja|熊野久須毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanokusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クマノクスビ}} (Kumanokusubi-no-Mikoto).
Amaterasu declared: "The five male deities are born from my jewel strings, so they are my children. The three female deities are born from your sword, so they are your children." Susanoo proclaimed: "My heart is pure, as proven by the birth of these goddesses." {{sfn|Kurano|1963|pp=34–39}}
=== Nihon Shoki=== The ''Nihon Shoki'' provides multiple accounts of the covenant, emphasizing variations in procedure and outcomes, but consistently affirming Susanoo's innocence.
== Interpretation == The differing accounts highlight nuanced perspectives on divine legitimacy and purity. In the ''Kojiki'', Amaterasu later declares the male deities as her children based on the origin of the divine objects, despite Susanoo’s prior declaration of victory. {{sfn|Ishii|2012|p=449}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Kojiki|author=Kurano, Kenji|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1963|isbn=4-00-300011-0}} * {{Cite book|title=Nihon Shoki|author=Sakamoto, Tarō|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1994|isbn=4-00-300041-2}}
